title: Tight Coupling
summary: Modules or services depend too heavily on each other, so a small change in one area can break many others.
inspection_goal: Identify parts of the system where dependency density, reciprocal relationships, or central chokepoints make change difficult and risky.
primary_signals: High fan-in and fan-out on the same node
Strongly connected components or direct cycles
Bridge nodes that connect otherwise separate parts of the graph
Dense dependency clusters with little boundary separation
inspection_questions: Which modules have both high incoming and outgoing dependency counts?
Where do cycles or reciprocal dependencies exist?
Which components act as dependency chokepoints?
Are there clusters that change together because of structural entanglement?
evidence_to_collect: DSM rows or columns with high density
recommendation_patterns: Split overloaded modules with mixed responsibilities
Break cycles using events, ports, or anti-corruption layers

severity_guidance.high: Critical paths contain cycles or multiple hotspots that block safe change.
severity_guidance.medium: Dependency pressure is concentrated but still locally manageable.
severity_guidance.low: Coupling is visible but bounded and explainable.
